title: Is This the Worst Overconsumption Trend of All Time?
description:
We've come a long way since the iconic "What's in My Bag" days of YouTube... Today, infuencers have hijacked a trend meant for self-expression and made it into a soulless and consumerist crisis. With excessive bag "restocks", entire handbag storage rooms, and contrived purse packing videos - Overconsumption is leaving us uninspired and unsatisfied. More importantly, it is causing us to lose our sense of individuality and destroying creativity. Let's discuss! Leave a *LIKE* if you enjoyed this video :-)

02:05 - 03:32 Ocean Bottle, our sponsor! 😍

TikTok’s “underconsumption core” is a fast-growing trend that challenges overconsumption, materialism, and performative sustainability by promoting mindful consumption, thrifting, and secondhand shopping. Today I break down how this movement reflects societal pressures and consumer fatigue while also offering a critique of the issues people have with it (as well as one of the overconsumption-driven culture we're all drowning in). As a former shopaholic, I explore how this shift can reshape our relationship with shopping and sustainability—and why I personally do NOT believe it's the same as minimalism, de-influencing etc!

title: How America Got So Good At Buying Sh*t
description:
Sign up and download Grammarly for FREE: http://grammarly.com/howmoneyworks

-----

Sign up for our FREE newsletter! - https://www.compoundeddaily.com/

Books we recommend - https://www.howmoneyworkslibrary.com/

-----

My Other Channel: @HowMoneyWorksUncut @HowHistoryWorks 

Edited By: Svibe Multimedia Studio

Music Courtesy of: Epidemic Sound

Select Footage Courtesy of: Getty Images

📩 Business Inquiries ➡️ sponsors@worksmedia.group

Sign up for our newsletter https://compoundeddaily.com 👈

All materials in these videos are for educational purposes only and fall within the guidelines of fair use. No copyright infringement intended. This video does not provide investment or financial advice of any kind.

#money #consumerism #wealth 

------

Americans are the best consumers on the planet and it’s really not even close. 
The runner up is China, and they spend less than a THIRD of what we do in any given year, even though they have almost five times as many people. 
It might not always feel like it, but by global standards we are incredibly rich, we LOVE buying shit, and we aren’t afraid of going into debt to keep doing it. 
More of our economy depends on the consumption of goods and services than basically any other major country around the world… but this has to have a limit right? 
As a direct result of our insatiable desire to consume, our household savings rates are now the lowest they have ever been, and high risk, high interest consumer lending has surpassed a trillion dollars. 
That doesn’t include things like car loans, home loans, student loans, medical loans or informal lending like buy now pay later, which are also approaching all time highs…
So what is going to happen to the best consumers on the planet, if they can’t afford to consume any more? And more importantly… could we solve all of our problems by just… buying less junk

title: Collecting VS Overconsumption: The BIG Problem
description:
Are you a careful collector... or an out-of-control consumer? Today we're breaking down the REAL differences between collecting and overconsuming. 

When does a collection BECOME overconsumption? How much stuff is TOO much?! Why is collecting okay, but overconsuming isn't?! 

From Needohs, Stanley Cups and Labubus to crazy body care and 'shower routine' collections: are influencers disguising their hoarding, impulse buying and trend-following as 'collecting'?

We also discuss how you can prevent your collecting from becoming overconsumption - by looking at some examples of 'collection content' on social media, Rach's own experience with impulsive purchasing and collecting, PLUS discussing some stories and comments from viewers! 

In today's video:
00:00 - Intro
01:51 - What's the difference?
07:04 - Case study: Lakenzo's dandelion crayon collection
08:18 - Can we judge other people's intent?
09:09 - Case study: mug collection by tatis_corner
13:13 - Are you REALLY 'collecting'?
14:35 - Bodycare collections... hoarding in disguise?
18:19 - Why is collecting okay but overconsumption isn't?
21:26 - Rach's big brain metaphor 
22:52 - How can we keep our collecting under control?
25:06 - The 'Exit Strategy' (+ video by thiccnesseverdeen)
29:26 - Reading your comments
36:33 - Final thoughts
